title: Gravitation as Geometric Tension of the Coordination Network and Cyclic Cosmology in YUCT
description: We present a mathematically rigorous derivation of gravity as an emergent phenomenon within the Yakushev Unified Coordination Theory (YUCT). Starting from the microscopic definition of coordination efficiency K_eff and the scalar field φ = ln(K_eff/K_0), we construct the Helmholtz free energy functional of the coordination network. All constants are expressed through parameters of the a priori dictionary. Variation of the action yields modified Einstein equations with a geometric tension tensor that replaces both dark matter and dark energy. The Newtonian limit is correctly derived, showing that the extra term acts as an effective dark matter density producing flat rotation curves. The cosmological constant Ω_Λ = 2/3 follows from the topology of the 19‑dimensional pre‑coordination space, and a cyclic cosmology emerges from a phase transition at K_eff = Φ (the golden ratio). Predictions are quantified for galactic and cosmological scales. The fine‑structure constant is derived as α⁻¹ = (3/2)^{12 + βγ·(2/3)} ≈ 137.036, eliminating a free parameter of the Standard Model. The fractal mass ladder m = m_e·q^{N_f} (q = (3/2)^{1/3}) is shown to span from elementary fermions to living cells. Thus geometric tension unifies dark components, quantum non‑locality, and the origin of fundamental constants under a single coordination principle.

structural_topology.description: This directory contains the multilingual versions (EN, RU, DE, ZH, AR, JA, KO) of the paper 'Gravitation as Geometric Tension of the Coordination Network and Cyclic Cosmology in YUCT'. The root main file is the English version, while the other languages are full translations of the same content.

derived_predictions.flat_rotation_curves: v_c²(∞) = α G₀ M_b with α ~ 10⁻⁷ (SI) → ~1 in Planck units

empirical_law_universality.beta_evidence: Over a dozen independent systems (atomic, biological, geophysical, cosmological) confirm β = 2/3 with uncertainties < 0.05
empirical_law_universality.domains_covered: Thermionic emission, chemical bond fluctuations, galaxy cluster correlations, mutation rates, metabolic scaling, city growth, GDP volatility, and more
empirical_law_universality.table_of_observations: Appendix L, Ferra1.2, and merged validation documents

notes: This is the complete multilingual documentation of the paper 'Gravitation as Geometric Tension of the Coordination Network and Cyclic Cosmology in YUCT', version 1.0. Each language file (EN, RU, DE, ZH, AR, JA, KO) is a full translation of the entire article, including all mathematical derivations, figures (fractal ladder, step‑wise effective modes), tables (SM parameter fixing), and references. The English version is the original reference. The Zenodo community for YUCT is 'yuct'. Multilingual metadata, fundamental constants (β=2/3, Ω_Λ=2/3, α⁻¹≈137.036, q=(3/2)^{1/3}), and theoretical predictions are explicitly declared in the 'custom' block; the primary metadata language is English.
